title: carex paupercula subsp. irrigua
intro: Carex paupercula subsp. irrigua is a subspecies of sedge, a type of grass-like perennial plant, commonly found in wetland areas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Native American cultures, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Carex paupercula subsp. irrigua are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, which are used topically to treat various skin conditions and internally to promote overall well-being.
